action.reasoning: Observation: The ensemble with CSRankNorm yielded a negative IC. In iteration 6, the best IC (0.003621) was achieved without CSRankNorm (i.e., training on raw labels) and with learning_rate=0.05, early_stopping_rounds=100. Analysis: CSRankNorm changes the target distribution to uniform ranks, which might not align well with the Pearson IC evaluation on raw returns in this specific setup. Training on raw returns directly seems to work better. Decision: I will remove CSRankNorm from learn_processors (training on raw labels) and use the ensemble of 5 LightGBM models with the hyperparameters from iteration 6 (learning_rate=0.05, early_stopping_rounds=100).
